evidence: The paper uses permutation importance for feature selection, defined as "the difference in the prediction error on the out-of-bag data when a given feature was permuted and unpermuted," not impurity-based importance. They validate results using partial dependency plots ("Figure 2 indicates how each variable influences the model predictions after we have accounted for the influence of the other four variables") and held-out validation cohorts with survival analysis demonstrating clinical utility. Since permutation importance is unbiased, the impurity importance bias does not apply.
